message:
feat(console): implement grapheme-cluster-aware str_width for emoji sequences

Replace the naive char-by-char summation in str_width() with a
cluster-walking algorithm that correctly measures display width for:

- ZWJ sequences (e.g., 👨‍👩‍👧‍👦 → width 2, not 8): the family
  emoji is four code points joined by U+200D zero-width joiners, but
  renders as a single double-width glyph in modern terminals.
- Regional indicator pairs (e.g., 🇺🇸 → width 2): two regional
  indicator symbols form a single flag glyph. An unpaired trailing
  indicator falls back to its individual char_width.
- Emoji presentation sequences (e.g., ❤️ → width 2): a text-default
  emoji followed by VS16 (U+FE0F) gains emoji presentation and
  occupies two columns.
- Keycap sequences (e.g., 1️⃣ → width 2): digit/hash/asterisk +
  VS16 + U+20E3 combining enclosing keycap.
- Emoji modifiers (skin tone selectors U+1F3FB–U+1F3FF): absorbed into
  the preceding emoji without adding width.
- Variation selectors and ZWJ as standalone: correctly return 0.

The algorithm peeks ahead to consume combining marks, variation
selectors, modifiers, and ZWJ+joined sequences into a single cluster
before emitting the cluster's width. This matches Unicode TR#11 / TR#51
terminal rendering expectations.

New helper functions: is_zero_width, is_zero_width_joiner,
is_variation_selector, is_emoji_modifier, is_regional_indicator,
is_emoji_presentation_candidate, is_keycap_base.

char_width() also gains a zero-width check before the combining check,
so ZWJ and variation selectors return 0 even when measured individually.

Added unit tests: zero-width scalars, family emoji cluster, heart
emoji, keycap, flag pair. Added matching E2E tests in the console
rendering suite.

Co-Authored-By: Claude Opus 4.6 (1M context) <noreply@anthropic.com>
